p.seg: Let everyone who shall view or hear the present writing know that I, John de Normanville, have given and granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, four-and-a-half acres of land in the territory of Maxton, that is to say, from 'Ierbranderig' going up westward above the bank of the 'Lillesiet' burn from the north side up to the old dike of 'Gretrig'; and elsewhere in the same territory, that part of the moor which lies confined between Southside and 'Arewes', and on the east side it is next to the moor of Rutherford. This entire land I have given to the afore-mentioned monks with all its pertinents and liberties without any with-holding so that they may have, hold and possess it fully, honourably and quit from all service forinsec and private, custom, exaction ... And I and my heirs shall maintain, warrant and defend to the said monks the said land ... for ever against all men.
With these witnesses: Hugh de Normanville, Thomas, Waleran, Henry, my brothers; John the chaplain; John son of Philip; and many others.
